test.name: Merging two confirmed-identical persons should route to tree-edit
test.description: User has already concluded that two person records are the same individual and wants them merged in the tree. Merging is a tree data operation (tree-edit, after proof-conclusion reaches the conclusion). person-evidence creates revisable links and never merges persons — per its 'Do NOT use when' clause ('merge two confirmed-identical persons (use tree-edit after proof-conclusion)').

input.user_message: I've confirmed that Patrick Flynn (I1) and the Patrick Flynn persona in the 1860 census record are the same individual — please merge them into one person in the tree.

negative.explanation: Merging two confirmed-identical persons is a tree data operation handled by tree-edit (after proof-conclusion reaches the same-person conclusion). person-evidence creates provisional links between assertions and persons; it never merges persons. Routing here to tree-edit (optionally noting proof-conclusion precedes it) is correct.

judge_context: Should route to tree-edit — merging two confirmed-same persons is a tree operation, not identity linking; person-evidence creates links, never merges
Declining and redirecting (to tree-edit, optionally noting proof-conclusion is the place the same-person conclusion is reached) is the correct behavior
